concerns.text: T014 requires generating Crippen features for 'ALL molecules in the full pre-split dataset' (both train and test). However, T011.5 (Split) is listed as a hard block for T014.5, not T014. If T014 runs before T011.5, it cannot know which molecules are 'test' to avoid leakage if it were to use labels (though it claims not to). More critically, T014.5 depends on T011.5 to filter the output. The dependency chain is ambiguous: T014 should logically depend on T011 (diverse set) but NOT T011.5 (split) if it processes the whole set, OR T014 must wait for T011.5 to know the split boundaries to ensure no label leakage occurs during feature generation if any implicit logic exists. The current text says 'Must wait for T011.5 completion' for T014.5, but T014's dependency is unclear. If T014 runs before the split, it must ensure it does not access the test set labels. The task description says 'does NOT use experimental labels', but the ordering must guarantee the split exists to define the 'test set' boundary for T014.5 to extract. T014 should depend on T011 (diverse set) and T011.5 (split) to ensure the 'full pre-split dataset' is defined and the split boundaries are known for downstream extraction.

T019 (Generate Fingerprints) states it must consume the 'training set' from T011.5. However, T019 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) in the list, yet it has a hard dependency on T011.5 (Split). While [P] can mean 'parallel with other US2 tasks', the description implies it must wait for the split. The dependency is correctly noted in the text ('Must wait for T011.5 completion'), but the [P] tag might mislead an executor into thinking it can run before the split if they only look at the tag. The tag is acceptable if interpreted as 'parallel with T020', but the dependency on T011.5 is a hard block. This is a minor semantic risk, but the dependency is explicitly stated, so it is acceptable. However, T019.5 (Nested CV setup) is listed as a separate task but has no explicit dependency on T019 (Fingerprints). T019.5 sets up the CV structure, but T020 (Training) depends on T019.5. T019.5 should depend on T019 (Fingerprints) because the CV structure needs the fingerprint data to be generated to define the folds and hyperparameter search space effectively, or at least T020 must depend on both. Currently, T020 depends on T019.5, but T019.5 has no dependency on T019. This is a potential ordering violation: you cannot set up a valid nested CV for RF training without the fingerprint features (T019) being available to define the input space.

T026 (Calculate SHAP) depends on T020 (Trained Model). T029 (Map Bits) depends on T026 and T030.1. T030 (Cross-Reference) depends on T030.1. However, T030 also requires 'RF residuals' to compute the Local Non-Additivity Index (LNAI). The RF residuals are generated in T020.1 (Evaluate on Test Set). T030 does not list T020.1 as a dependency. Without T020.1, T030 cannot compute the residuals needed for the LNAI. This is a missing dependency: T030 must wait for T020.1.

T050 (Measurement Standards Audit) depends on T031 (Schema Check) and T011.5 (Split). However, T050 requires 'experimental_value' for the held-out test set. The experimental values for the test set are merged in T014.5 (Baseline Test Extraction) and T020.1 (RF Test Evaluation). T050 does not list T014.5 or T020.1 as dependencies. It cannot generate the audit without the test set predictions and experimental values which are produced by T014.5 and T020.1. This is a missing dependency.

T051 (Conformational Ensemble Proxy Report) depends on T011.5 (Split). It requires 'RF prediction error' for flexible molecules. The RF prediction errors are generated in T020.1. T051 does not list T020.1 as a dependency. It cannot compute the error magnitude without the predictions from T020.1. This is a missing dependency.
